message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Bizarre Lineage Map: All NPC Locations & Fast Travel Guide 2026",
  description: "Master the Bizarre Lineage map with our 2026 guide. Find all NPC locations, raid bosses, fighting style trainers, and fast travel bus stops in Morioh Cho.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e1b4b/fff?text=Bizarre+Lineage+Map+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Bizarre Lineage Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Bizarre Lineage Map**: There is no in-game mini-map; players must rely on landmarks and bus stops for navigation.
- **Fast Travel**: Use the **Rhett NPC** found at every Bus Stop to teleport across Morioh Cho instantly.
- **NPC Navigation**: Press the **N Key** to highlight NPCs (Purple), Enemies (Red), and Bus Stops (Green).
- **Core Progression**: Always complete the **Morioh Grand Hotel tutorial** first for a 1.5x XP multiplier.
- **Prestige Location**: The Arch Mage is located in the **Hospital behind Bus Stop 10** for late-game resets.
</Callout>

Navigating the sprawling, neon-lit streets of Morioh Cho can be a daunting task for any new Stand user, especially since the game intentionally omits a traditional in-game radar or mini-map. Mastering the **bizarre lineage map** and its various landmarks is the difference between efficient leveling and aimless wandering through dangerous alleyways. This comprehensive 2026 guide provides a deep-dive breakdown of every essential NPC location, from humble quest givers to legendary raid bosses, ensuring you never lose your way in this JJBA-inspired world. By understanding the intricacies of the **bizarre lineage map** and its fast-travel bus stop system, you can optimize your progression, locate hidden trainers, and reach the late-game prestige ranks faster than ever before. Whether you are looking for Tonio’s cafe or the elusive Arch Mage, we have mapped out every corner of Morioh to make your journey smoother and more rewarding.

<Section icon={Map} title="Mastering Navigation and Fast Travel">

Since Bizarre Lineage does not provide a visual map on your HUD, you must learn to navigate using the Bus Stop system. Morioh is divided into several zones, each connected by a main road. Walking between these zones can take several minutes, which is why the Fast Travel mechanic is the most important tool in your arsenal.

The key to finding your way is the **N Key** navigation tool. This built-in radar highlights specific entities through walls, making it easier to spot NPCs even if they are hidden behind buildings or trees.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="Green Markers">
    - **Bus Stop NPCs**
    - Indicates fast travel points
    - Use for world navigation
  </Card>
  <Card title="Purple Markers">
    - **Important NPCs**
    - Quest givers and trainers
    - Shopkeepers and raid starters
  </Card>
  <Card title="Red Markers">
    - **Hostile Enemies**
    - Quest targets and mobs
    - Useful for farming EXP
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

<Callout type="tip" title="Fast Travel Strategy">
Always look for the **Rhett NPC** at every bus stop. Interacting with her allows you to teleport to any other stop you have discovered. If you are lost, find the nearest road and follow it until you see a green marker on your N-key radar.
</Callout>

| NPC Name | Primary Function | Map Location | Bus Stop |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Rhett** | Fast Travel Teleport | Every Bus Stop | All Stops |
| **Receptionist** | Initial Tutorial | Morioh Grand Hotel | Bus Stop 1 |
| **Gupta** | Item Crafting | Left of Morioh Station | Bus Stop 1 |
| **Rahaj** | Money Store/Pawn | Multiple (Convenience Star) | Stops 1, 2, 3 |

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Quest NPC Locations and Story Progression">

Progressing through the **bizarre lineage map** requires you to complete storyline quests in a specific order. If you attempt to speak to a late-game NPC before completing earlier requirements, they will simply ignore you. Side quests, however, can be picked up at any time to supplement your experience gains.

Most early-game quests are concentrated around Bus Stop 1 and the Morioh Station area. As you grow stronger, the story will pull you toward the outskirts of the city and eventually the graveyard or the hospital.

<Callout type="warning" title="Quest Sequence">
Storyline quests must be completed in order. If an NPC gives you a sassy remark, check your quest log to ensure you haven't skipped a previous step in the Morioh Grand Hotel or at the Station.
</Callout>

| NPC Name | Purpose | Bus Stop | Specific Landmark |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Akihiko** | Josuke Questline | Bus Stop 1 | Behind a pillar at Morioh Station |
| **Tonio Trussardi** | Recipe Side Quest | Bus Stop 1 | Inside Cafe de Maigot |
| **Josuke** | Main Story Giver | Bus Stop 1 | Near the Station entrance |
| **Mission Board** | PvE Daily Tasks | Bus Stop 1 | Near the main plaza |

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Fighting Style Trainers and Stand Growth">

Your combat effectiveness depends on your Stand and your Fighting Style. While Stands are obtained through Arrows, Fighting Styles must be learned from specific trainers hidden throughout the **bizarre lineage map**. These trainers often require a fee or a specific task before they will teach you their secrets.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Get a Stand Arrow">
    Complete early story quests or find random world spawns to obtain your first Stand Arrow. Use it to unlock your core abilities.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Locate a Trainer">
    Travel to the specific Bus Stop associated with your desired style (e.g., Boxing at Stop 2 or Karate at Stop 3).
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Train Conjuration">
    Once you have your kit, head to the **Gym Mat** to meditate. This is the most consistent way to unlock Stand abilities fast.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Callout type="success" title="Style Synergy">
Focus on your Stand's Destructive Power first. Once your core damage is stable, add a Fighting Style to complement your combos. Stacking too many systems early can spread your stats too thin.
</Callout>

| Fighting Style | Trainer Name | Bus Stop | Location Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Boxing** | Boxing Coach | Bus Stop 2 | Jojo's Bizarre Gym (Top Floor) |
| **Karate** | Karate Sensei | Bus Stop 3 | Left of the park, near the pond |
| **Vampirism** | Elder Vampire | Bus Stop 15 | Castle on the skull-horned hill |

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Raid Bosses and Special NPCs">

Raids are the primary method for farming high-tier items like Lucky Arrows and Raid Tokens. These NPCs send you to instanced zones where you must face powerful bosses or waves of enemies. Because raids are difficult, it is highly recommended to team up with other players before interacting with these NPCs.

<Callout type="info" title="Raid Rewards">
Higher performance ranks in raids result in better loot. Dying during a raid significantly reduces your final rank, so prioritize survival over raw damage output when starting out.
</Callout>

| Raid Boss | NPC Name | Bus Stop | Location Details |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Yoshikage Kira** | Yoshikage Kira | Bus Stop 1 | Left of Morioh Station, behind a tree |
| **Jotaro Kujo** | Chumbo | Bus Stop 2 | Left side entrance of Jojo's Bizarre Gym |
| **Muhammad Avdol** | Avdol | Bus Stop 14 | Located near the beach shoreline |
| **Pucci (Evo)** | Pucci | Bus Stop 18 | Inside the chapel at the graveyard |

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Endgame Progression and Prestige">

Once you reach the level cap, your journey on the **bizarre lineage map** isn't over. The Prestige system allows you to reset your level in exchange for permanent power increases, Prestige Shards, and additional Stand storage. This is essential for players looking to master multiple Stands or optimize their builds for PvP.

<Checklist
  id="prestige-requirements"
  title="Prestige Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Reach the current maximum level",
    "Accumulate 10,000 Cash",
    "Travel to the Hospital at Bus Stop 10",
    "Speak with the Arch Mage NPC",
    "Confirm the reset to earn Prestige Shards"
  ]}
/>

<Callout type="tip" title="Prestige Timing">
Do not rush into your first Prestige the moment you hit the level cap. Ensure your current Stand is fully developed and you have enough resources to quickly level up again, as your level will return to 1.
</Callout>

| Endgame Goal | Required NPC | Bus Stop | Location |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Prestige Reset** | Arch Mage | Bus Stop 10 | Inside the Hospital building |
| **C-Moon / MIH** | Pucci | Bus Stop 18 | Graveyard Chapel |
| **Legendary Chests** | Gupta | Bus Stop 1 | Near Morioh Station |

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Bizarre Lineage Map FAQ">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "How do I find a specific NPC if I am lost?",
    answer: "The best way is to use the N Key to scan for Purple markers. If you don't see any, find the nearest Bus Stop and check the sign for the stop number, then use this guide to correlate the number with the NPC you need."
  },
  {
    question: "Where is the best place to farm Stand Arrows on the map?",
    answer: "Early-game players should focus on completing NPC quests near Bus Stop 1, as many provide Stand Arrows as rewards. You can also find them as random world spawns or by farming PvE Mission Boards."
  },
  {
    question: "Is there a way to see the full Bizarre Lineage map in-game?",
    answer: "No, there is currently no in-game map item or HUD element. Players must memorize the Bus Stop locations and landmarks like the Gym, Hospital, and Station to navigate effectively."
  },
  {
    question: "What does the Rhett NPC do?",
    answer: "Rhett is the fast travel NPC. She is located at every Bus Stop along the road and allows you to teleport to any other Bus Stop for a small fee, which is essential for traversing the large Morioh Cho map."
  }
]} />

</Section>
